metadata.seed: Yeah, last week I was on my way to class and my girlfriend actually called me up saying that I forgot to take out the trash. And I kind of started thinking, wow, like, I forgot to take out the trash. Something so simple, a simple chore. All I had to do was take out the trash today and I forgot to do it. And then I started to think, well, I can't do something as simple as that. Why is she still with me? Or, and why, why can't I do that? Why can't I do a simple task? Am I good enough for anyone? Am I good enough for her? Kind of starting to feel a little fearful that she would just up and leave me.

patient.query: Yeah, last week I was on my way to class and my girlfriend actually called me up saying that I forgot to take out the trash. And then I started to think, well, I can't do something as simple as that. Why is she still with me? Or, and why, why can't I do that? Why can't I do a simple task? Am I good enough for anyone? Am I good enough for her? Kind of starting to feel a little fearful that she would just up and leave me.

triggers: forgot to take out the trash

validate_and_reflect.response: It sounds like that phone call really shook you up, and it brought up some pretty intense worries about yourself and your relationship. It makes sense that you'd feel fearful when thoughts like that start swirling around.

patient.query: Yeah, it feels like such a small thing, but it just spirals. I don't know, maybe I've always felt like I'm not doing enough or like I’m letting people down… especially when I think about my family. They have these high expectations, and I just... I don't always know how to measure up, you know? It’s like everything I do is under this microscope.

automatic_thoughts: I don't know how to measure up
everything I do is under a microscope

neutral: Fear of being a burden to others (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds incredibly frustrating to feel like your actions are constantly being scrutinized, especially when it comes to your family. Can you tell me more about what specifically makes you feel like you're not measuring up to their expectations?

validate_and_reflect.response: It sounds incredibly frustrating to feel like your actions are constantly being scrutinized, especially when it comes to your family. It takes courage to acknowledge those feelings of not measuring up.

socratic_questioning.response: It sounds incredibly frustrating to feel like your actions are constantly being scrutinized, especially when it comes to your family. Can you tell me more about what specifically makes you feel like you're not measuring up to their expectations?

alternative_perspective.response: It sounds incredibly frustrating to feel like your actions are constantly being scrutinized, especially when it comes to family. What do you think someone who cares about you might say about your efforts and how you're measuring up?

safety_check.response: It sounds incredibly distressing to feel like your actions are constantly being scrutinized, especially when thinking about your family. When you said that, were you having any thoughts of hurting yourself or not wanting to be here?

patient.query: I guess it’s just this feeling that no matter what I do, it’s never quite enough. Like when I got that promotion at work, I thought they would be proud, but instead, it was more about what I should be doing next. And then there’s this pressure to always have it all together, you know? It makes me anxious, like I’m just waiting for them to point out what I’m doing wrong.

triggers: pressure to have it all together
automatic_thoughts: no matter what I do, it’s never quite enough
waiting for them to point out what I’m doing wrong

validate_and_reflect.response: It sounds like you're experiencing a lot of pressure and anxiety, especially around your accomplishments and feeling like you need to constantly prove yourself. That feeling of never quite measuring up can be really tough.

patient.query: Yeah, I mean, I could just feel it in their tone, like they were more interested in what I’d achieve next rather than acknowledging what I just accomplished. It feels like there’s this unspoken rule that once you hit one goal, it’s time to move on to the next, and, honestly, it just makes me feel kind of invisible. I wish I could just share a moment of happiness, but it’s like I can’t shake off that nagging sense of not measuring up

automatic_thoughts: they are more interested in future achievements

patient.query: I think... they’d probably say I should focus on the positives, or that I should be proud of myself. But honestly, I just feel stuck in this loop of comparison. It’s hard to take in what they say when the expectation feels so heavy. Sometimes I wonder if they really see me or just what I can achieve.

automatic_thoughts: they’d probably say I should focus on the positives
I should be proud of myself
I wonder if they really see me or just what I can achieve

patient.query: It’s like every time I think I’m finally getting things right, there’s this voice in my head saying, “What if this isn’t enough?” I guess I’m always trying to gauge their reactions, and it feels exhausting. I just want to feel accepted for who I am, not just what I can do. But when those expectations are there, it’s like I can’t breathe, and I end up second-guessing everything.

neutral: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're carrying a lot of pressure to meet expectations, and that inner voice can be really draining. Can you tell me more about where you think that "What if this isn't enough?" thought comes from?

validate_and_reflect.response: It sounds like you're carrying a lot of pressure to perform and be perfect, and that’s understandably exhausting. It takes courage to acknowledge that desire for acceptance beyond what you do.

socratic_questioning.response: It sounds like you're carrying a lot of pressure to meet expectations, and that inner voice can be really draining. Can you tell me more about where you think that "What if this isn't enough?" thought comes from?

alternative_perspective.response: It sounds incredibly draining to constantly question whether you're "enough," and to feel like you're performing for others. What do you think someone who truly accepted you would notice about you, beyond what you do?

safety_check.response: That sounds incredibly draining, and it makes sense that you'd feel exhausted. When you say you're second-guessing everything, were you having any thoughts of hurting yourself or not wanting to be here?

patient.query: I don’t know… maybe it’s from when I was younger? I remember my parents always pushing me to achieve more, like it was never just about being happy, but about being the best. I think they meant well, but sometimes it felt like my worth was tied to my accomplishments. I guess that’s just stuck with me. Even now, when things don’t go perfectly, that voice pops up, and it makes it hard to enjoy anything at all.

automatic_thoughts: my worth is tied to accomplishments

patient.query: Well, it usually happens during family gatherings or when I’m talking to my parents. Like, they’ll ask about my job or my plans, and I can feel that weight creeping in. Even small comments can trigger it, and suddenly I’m stuck feeling like I need to prove something. And honestly, even at work sometimes, when my boss gives feedback, it feels like the stakes are so high, and I just start overthinking it all. It’s exhausting trying to navigate those moments.

neutral: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)
Fear of being a burden to others (finding)
